findings.title: Notification Priority Levels Implementation
findings.description: Implementing a three-tier priority system for notifications requires updates to the notification rendering logic and user interface components. This will also necessitate changes to the backend to support priority levels.
assumptions: The current notification system can be modified to support priority levels.

evidence.excerpt: Propose a three-tier priority system that visually differentiates urgency.

recommendations: Design and implement backend changes to support priority levels.

findings.title: Android 14 Notification Delivery Failure
findings.description: Push notifications are failing on Android 14 devices, affecting 8% of users. This is a critical issue that needs to be resolved to ensure reliable notification delivery.
assumptions: The issue is solely related to Android 14 and not a broader platform issue.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate and resolve the notification delivery issue on Android 14.

findings.title: ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: Implementing ML-based notification batching will require developing a machine learning model that learns user preferences. This is a complex task that involves data collection and model training.
assumptions: Sufficient user engagement data is available for training the ML model.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Plan for data collection and model training as part of the implementation.

findings.title: User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Creating a user preference page requires a new API endpoint and a React component. This will involve backend and frontend development efforts.
assumptions: The existing backend can support new API endpoints.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Develop the new preferences API and React component.

findings.title: Notification Delivery Latency Issue
findings.description: The current notification delivery latency exceeds the SLO of 200ms, which could lead to user dissatisfaction. This requires architectural changes to the delivery pipeline.
assumptions: The proposed architectural changes can be implemented without major disruptions.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Implement async writes and batch inserts to improve latency.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with the SmartNotify project. Key components include implementing notification priority levels, addressing Android 14 delivery failures, and improving notification delivery latency. A phased delivery plan is proposed with MVP and V1 focusing on immediate user needs and V2 targeting advanced features like ML-based batching.
